about.disclaimerP1: ChartingPath bietet Tools und Bildungsinhalte nur zu Informationszwecken. Wir bieten keine Anlageberatung an, und jeder Handel birgt Risiken.
about.title: Über ChartingPath
about.transparencyDesc: Wir glauben an vollständige Transparenz. Von unserer Preisgestaltung bis hin zu unseren Methoden sind wir offen über alles. Keine versteckten Gebühren, keine falschen Versprechungen – nur ehrlicher, unkomplizierter Service.
about.disclaimerP2: Die Performance in der Vergangenheit ist keine Garantie für zukünftige Ergebnisse. Führen Sie immer Ihre eigenen Recherchen durch und berücksichtigen Sie Ihre Risikobereitschaft, bevor Sie Handelsentscheidungen treffen.
about.subtitle: Wir unterstützen Trader weltweit mit professionellen Tools, Schulungen und Technologie, um nachhaltigen Erfolg an den Finanzmärkten zu erzielen.
about.missionP2: Wir glauben, dass mit den richtigen Tools, dem richtigen Wissen und der richtigen Unterstützung jeder ein erfolgreicher Trader werden kann. Unsere Mission ist es, die Eintrittsbarrieren abzubauen und erstklassige Ressourcen bereitzustellen, die einst nur institutionellen Tradern zur Verfügung standen.
about.communityDesc: Trading muss nicht einsam sein. Wir fördern eine unterstützende Community, in der Trader voneinander lernen, Erkenntnisse austauschen und gemeinsam auf ihrem Trading-Weg wachsen können.
about.visionP1: Die weltweit vertrauenswürdigste Plattform für Retail-Trader zu werden, anerkannt für Innovation, herausragende Bildung und unerschütterliches Engagement für den Erfolg unserer Nutzer auf allen globalen Märkten.
about.visionP2: Wir stellen uns eine Zukunft vor, in der jeder Trader Zugang zu Tools und Wissen in institutioneller Qualität hat, wodurch gleiche Wettbewerbsbedingungen auf den Finanzmärkten geschaffen und eine globale Gemeinschaft informierter, disziplinierter und erfolgreicher Trader gefördert wird.
about.valuesSubtitle: Diese Prinzipien leiten alles, was wir tun, und prägen unseren Service für Trader weltweit
about.educationFirstDesc: Wissen ist Macht im Trading. Wir priorisieren eine umfassende Ausbildung und bieten klare Erklärungen, praktische Beispiele und kontinuierliche Lernmöglichkeiten, damit Sie als Trader wachsen können.
about.excellenceDesc: Wir streben nach Exzellenz in jedem Tool, jeder Funktion und jedem Inhalt, den wir erstellen. Unser Qualitätsanspruch stellt sicher, dass Sie professionelle Ressourcen erhalten, die einen echten Mehrwert bieten.
about.globalAccessibilityDesc: Wir bedienen Trader weltweit, unabhängig von Standort oder Erfahrungsstand. Unsere Plattform ist so konzipiert, dass sie für Trader in allen Märkten und Zeitzonen zugänglich, inklusiv und wertvoll ist.
about.missionP1: Den Zugang zu professionellen Trading-Tools und -Schulungen zu demokratisieren, um Tradern aller Niveaus in die Lage zu versetzen, fundierte Entscheidungen zu treffen, Risiken effektiv zu managen und ihre finanziellen Ziele durch systematische und datengesteuerte Ansätze zu erreichen.
about.innovationDesc: Die Märkte entwickeln sich weiter, und wir auch. Wir sind ständig innovativ und integrieren die neuesten Technologien und Trading-Methoden, um Sie an der Spitze der Marktchancen zu halten.
about.commitmentDesc: Ob Sie nun Ihre ersten Schritte im Trading unternehmen oder ein erfahrener Profi sind, ChartingPath hat sich verpflichtet, Ihnen die Werkzeuge, das Wissen und die Unterstützung zu bieten, die Sie für Ihren Erfolg benötigen. Wir sind nicht nur eine Plattform – wir sind Ihr Partner auf dem Weg zu Trading-Exzellenz.

faq.refundA3: Bitte beachte, dass Benutzer, die eine Rückbuchungs-/Streitanfrage oder einen Anspruch eingereicht haben, keinen Anspruch auf eine Rückerstattung haben.
faq.platformQ: Mit welchen Plattformen funktionieren die Skripte?
faq.upgradeQ: Wie kann ich ein Upgrade durchführen, ohne zu bezahlen?
faq.billingQ: Wie wirkt sich ein Upgrade meines Plans auf die Abrechnung aus?
faq.upgradeA1: Du kannst dein Jahresabonnement jederzeit ohne zusätzliche Transaktionen upgraden. Alle verbleibenden Tage deines aktuellen Plans werden automatisch in gleichwertige Werttage des neuen Tarifs umgewandelt. Du verlierst nichts, sondern nutzt den verbleibenden Betrag, um zu einer besseren Version zu wechseln. Dadurch verkürzt sich die verbleibende Laufzeit deines Abonnements und dein nächster Zahlungstermin verschiebt sich.
faq.upgradeA2: Wenn du ein Downgrade durchführst, bleibt dein aktueller Plan bis zum Ablaufdatum des Abonnements unverändert. Danach, nach Ablauf, wird dein neuer, heruntergestufter Plan zum ausgewählten Tarif aktiv.
faq.refreshQ: Wie oft werden die Musterdaten aktualisiert?
faq.upgradeA3: Bitte beachte, dass dies keine kostenlose Testversion ist. Wenn du ein Upgrade durchführst, gibt es keine Möglichkeit, diese Aktion rückgängig zu machen, obwohl du einen heruntergestuften Plan für den nächsten Abrechnungszeitraum einrichten kannst.
faq.cancelA2: Gekündigte Testversionen werden sofort nach der Kündigung beendet.
faq.scriptLibA: Gebrauchsfertige Trading-Skripte für Pine Script (TradingView), Python (MT4/MT5) und MQL5. Jedes Skript enthält Einrichtungsanweisungen, Backtest-Ergebnisse und Anpassungsanleitungen.
faq.scriptLibQ: Was ist in der Skriptbibliothek enthalten?
faq.refresh15m: Aktualisierungen alle 15 Minuten für die Top 300 der liquidesten Ticker
faq.refreshDaily: Aktualisierungen alle 24 Stunden für alle 8.500+ Ticker
faq.cancelA1: Sie können Ihr Abonnement jederzeit kündigen, und es wird nach Ablauf der aktuellen bezahlten Laufzeit nicht automatisch verlängert. Der bezahlte Dienst bleibt für die Dauer der bezahlten Laufzeit aktiv.
faq.refresh4h: Aktualisierungen alle 4 Stunden für alle 8.500+ Ticker
faq.platformA: Unsere Skripte sind kompatibel mit TradingView (Pine Script), MetaTrader 4/5 (Python und MQL) und den meisten wichtigen Handelsplattformen.
faq.refresh1h: Stündliche Aktualisierungen für die wichtigsten 1.100 Ticker. Aktualisierungen alle 8 Stunden für das erweiterte Universum
faq.billingA: Wenn Sie auf einen höheren Plan upgraden, berechnen wir automatisch die anteilige Differenz und verrechnen Ihren verbleibenden Abonnementwert mit dem neuen Plan. Ihr Abrechnungszyklus wird entsprechend angepasst und das aktualisierte Datum der nächsten Zahlung wird in Ihrem Konto angezeigt.
faq.refreshA: Die Aktualisierungshäufigkeit der Daten hängt von Ihrer Planstufe und dem Zeitrahmen ab, den Sie analysieren.
faq.refreshNote: Eine höhere Aktualisierungsfrequenz für Intraday-Daten erfordert erhebliche API-Ressourcen. Premium-Stufen erhalten während Perioden mit hohem Datenverkehr vorrangigen Zugriff.
faq.refundA2: Für Upgrades auf teurere Pläne, Monatspläne oder Marktdaten können wir keine Rückerstattungen anbieten, auch nicht am selben Tag, an dem die Zahlung erfolgt ist.
faq.refundA1: Rückerstattungen gelten nur für Jahrespläne und müssen innerhalb von 14 Kalendertagen nach der Zahlung beantragt werden. Um eine Rückerstattung zu beantragen, kontaktiere bitte unser Support-Team.

screener.q_methodology: What methodology does ChartingPath use for pattern detection?
screener.a_methodologyIntro: ChartingPath uses a three-layer detection pipeline based on institutional-grade standards established by Thomas Bulkowski's pattern research.
screener.a_layer1Desc: The engine scans price action using adaptive lookback windows (up to 120 bars) with peak/trough detection to identify structural formations. Each pattern must satisfy strict formation rules before being registered.
screener.a_layer2Desc: Every detection passes through a 6-factor weighted validation model analyzing trend alignment (30%), RSI (15%), MACD momentum (15%), ADX strength (15%), volume conviction (15%), and risk calibration (10%). Patterns must score above the confirmation threshold to proceed.
screener.a_layer3Desc: Confirmed patterns are validated against the next higher timeframe (e.g., 4H pattern → Daily check). The model evaluates HTF trend direction (40%), S/R proximity (25%), HTF momentum via MACD + RSI (20%), and cross-TF volume profile (15%). Patterns trading against strong higher-timeframe opposition are rejected.
screener.a_methodologyResult: Result: Patterns must pass all three layers to appear in the Screener, Chart overlays, and research views. Only fully confirmed patterns are displayed — significantly reducing false positives.
screener.q_patternDefinitions: How is each pattern identified? (Full definitions)
screener.a_patternDefsIntro: Below are the structural rules enforced by ChartingPath's Bulkowski Engine for each of the 15 supported patterns.
screener.a_doubleTopDesc: Two peaks at approximately the same price level (±1.5% tolerance) separated by a trough. Requires a prior uptrend of ≥2%. Confirmed on a break below the neckline (trough level).
screener.a_tripleTopDesc: Three peaks at similar price levels (±1.5%) with two intervening troughs. Requires a prior uptrend of ≥2%. Confirmed on a break below the lowest trough (neckline).
screener.a_headShouldersDesc: Three peaks where the middle peak (Head) is higher than both outer peaks (Shoulders). Shoulders must be approximately symmetric (within 30% height tolerance). Requires a prior uptrend of ≥3%. Confirmed on a neckline break.
screener.a_risingWedgeDesc: Converging trendlines with both support and resistance sloping upward, with resistance rising at a shallower angle. Requires ≥3 touches on each trendline. Bearish breakdown expected.
screener.a_doubleBottomDesc: Two troughs at approximately the same price level (±1.5% tolerance) separated by a peak. Requires a prior downtrend of ≥2%. Confirmed on a break above the neckline (peak level).
screener.a_tripleBottomDesc: Three troughs at similar price levels (±1.5%) with two intervening peaks. Requires a prior downtrend of ≥2%. Confirmed on a break above the highest peak (neckline).
screener.a_inverseHSDesc: Three troughs where the middle trough (Head) is lower than both outer troughs (Shoulders). Symmetric shoulder tolerance of 30%. Requires a prior downtrend of ≥3%. Confirmed on a neckline breakout.
screener.a_fallingWedgeDesc: Converging trendlines with both support and resistance sloping downward, with support falling at a shallower angle. Requires ≥3 touches on each trendline. Bullish breakout expected.
screener.a_bullFlagDesc: A sharp upward move (flagpole, ≥5% gain) followed by a tight, downward-sloping consolidation channel. The flag body should retrace no more than 50% of the pole. ADX > 20 required. Bullish continuation on upside break.
screener.a_bearFlagDesc: A sharp downward move (flagpole, ≥5% drop) followed by a tight, upward-sloping consolidation channel. The flag body should retrace no more than 50% of the pole. ADX > 20 required. Bearish continuation on downside break.
screener.a_cupHandleDesc: A rounded bottom (cup) followed by a shallow pullback (handle). The cup must form over at least 7 bars with a prior uptrend of ≥5%. The handle should retrace less than 50% of the cup depth. Bullish breakout above the rim.
screener.a_ascTriangleDesc: Flat resistance with rising support. Requires ≥3 touches on each trendline. Higher lows converge toward a horizontal ceiling. Bullish breakout expected above resistance.
screener.a_descTriangleDesc: Flat support with declining resistance. Requires ≥3 touches on each trendline. Lower highs converge toward a horizontal floor. Bearish breakdown expected below support.
screener.a_donchianLongDesc: Price closes above the highest high of the past N bars (Donchian channel). Requires ADX > 20 for trend confirmation. No excessive retracement from recent highs. Bullish momentum signal.
screener.a_donchianShortDesc: Price closes below the lowest low of the past N bars (Donchian channel). Requires ADX > 20 for trend confirmation. No excessive retracement from recent lows. Bearish momentum signal.
screener.q_qualityScoring: What is the quality scoring system?
screener.a_qualityScoringIntro: Each detected pattern receives a quality grade (A through F) based on a 9-factor weighted model:
screener.a_scoreB: Score 6–8: Solid formation with minor deviations
screener.a_scoreC: Score 4–6: Acceptable but with notable imperfections
screener.a_scoreDF: Score 0–4: Marginal formation, use with caution
screener.q_repeatabilityGate: What is the Repeatability Gate and how does it affect grades?
screener.a_repeatabilityIntro: Beyond the 9-factor quality score, each grade must also pass a statistical repeatability gate. This ensures top-tier grades reflect not just visual quality, but proven historical reliability. A pattern that scores 9/10 on form but has zero historical trades is hard-capped at C-grade.
screener.a_repeatADesc: Weighted score ≥ 7.5 AND ≥ 30 historical occurrences AND ≥ 50% win rate AND positive expectancy (> 0R). All four conditions must be met — missing any one caps the grade at B.
screener.a_repeatBDesc: Weighted score ≥ 6.0 AND ≥ 15 historical occurrences AND positive expectancy (> 0R). Patterns with strong form but insufficient sample size (15–29 trades) are capped here.
screener.a_repeatCDesc: Weighted score ≥ 4.5. No minimum sample size required. This is the maximum grade for patterns with insufficient or no historical data — the system will not promote data-poor patterns above C regardless of visual quality.
screener.a_repeatDDesc: Weighted score ≥ 3.0 for D-grade. Below 3.0 receives F-grade. These patterns have notable structural deficiencies and should be used with extreme caution.
screener.a_repeatabilityNote: Why this matters: Many platforms assign grades purely on visual pattern quality. A symmetrical Double Bottom on an exotic ticker might look perfect, but if that pattern has never worked historically on that instrument class, a high grade would be misleading. The repeatability gate prevents this by requiring statistical proof before awarding top-tier grades.
screener.q_dataPoorFallback: How does ChartingPath handle instruments with limited historical data?
screener.a_dataPoorIntro: For newer or exotic instruments where per-symbol historical data is sparse, the platform uses a 4-level fallback chain to prevent data gaps from creating blind spots:
screener.a_fallback1Desc: If ≥ 30 resolved trades exist for the exact pattern + symbol + timeframe combo, those stats are used directly. This is the highest-confidence source.
screener.a_fallback2Desc: If the specific timeframe lacks data, the system checks adjacent timeframes (e.g., 1h → 4h → 8h → 1d → 1wk) for the same pattern + symbol. Stats are tagged as cross-timeframe.
screener.a_fallback3Desc: If per-symbol data is insufficient (< 5 trades), the system pools all instruments that have traded the same pattern to compute an aggregate win rate and expectancy. Scores are tagged 'Est.' in the UI.
screener.a_fallback4Desc: When even pattern-level aggregates lack data, a neutral Bayesian prior is applied (50% assumed win rate with a virtual 10-sample prior). This produces a conservative ~40% Analyst score rather than 0%. Tagged 'Prior' in the UI.
screener.a_dataPoorNote: Transparency: The source of every score is visible in the UI. Scores derived from aggregates or priors show 'Est.' or 'Prior' badges with tooltips explaining the data source. The Agent Scoring table clearly distinguishes between proven, estimated, and prior-based scores so you always know the confidence level behind each number.
screener.q_validationPipeline: What does the validation pipeline filter out?
screener.a_validationIntro: Each detection must survive three sequential layers. Rejection at any layer removes the pattern from all views:
screener.a_valLayer1Desc: Structural geometry must match Bulkowski's formation rules (prior trend, touch counts, symmetry)
screener.a_valLayer2Desc: Composite score ≥ 0.15 required (6-factor: trend, RSI, MACD, ADX, volume, risk). Rejected if ≤ −0.15
screener.a_valLayer3Desc: Higher-timeframe trend, S/R proximity, momentum, and volume must not oppose the pattern direction. Rejected if score ≤ −0.20
screener.a_fullyConfirmedDesc: Pattern passes all 3 layers → displayed in Screener, Charts, and Research views
screener.a_validationResult: This three-layer pipeline ensures only patterns with structural validity, technical context support, and multi-timeframe alignment reach the user — providing institutional-grade signal quality.
screener.q_evaluation: How does ChartingPath evaluate pattern strategies at institutional level?
screener.a_evaluationIntro: ChartingPath applies a 5-module institutional audit framework to every strategy evaluation, addressing gaps commonly found in retail platforms (MT4/MT5).
screener.a_eval1Desc: All expectancy figures deduct estimated spread, slippage, and commission costs. Gross R-multiples are adjusted per asset class (e.g., FX: ~0.055R/trade, Stocks: ~0.04R/trade). Strategies must remain profitable after costs.
screener.a_eval2Desc: Rolling-window analysis tracks how win rates and R-multiples evolve over time. A negative slope with R² > 0.3 flags a decaying edge — suggesting market adaptation or regime shift.
screener.a_eval3Desc: When multiple patterns fire on the same instrument within 5 bars, they're flagged as a signal cluster. Clustering inflates apparent diversification — 3 clustered trades = 1 correlated bet, not 3 independent ones.
screener.a_eval4Desc: 1,000 random resamples of actual trade outcomes generate a distribution of equity curves. This reveals the probability of ruin, 95th-percentile max drawdown, and how lucky/unlucky the historical sequence was.
screener.a_eval5Desc: A composite grade combining: net-positive expectancy, edge stability, clustering rate, and ruin probability. CONDITIONAL strategies require additional monitoring; FAIL strategies should not be traded.
screener.a_evaluationNote: Why this matters: MT4/MT5 strategy reports often show inflated ROI because they ignore transaction costs, don't test for edge decay, and present a single (potentially lucky) equity curve. ChartingPath's audit framework provides the institutional rigor needed to distinguish real edges from overfitted artifacts.
screener.q_agentProofGate: What is the Agent Scoring Proof Gate?
screener.a_agentProofGateIntro: The Agent Scoring system uses a Proof Gate to separate statistically validated signals from unproven ones. This prevents the system from assigning confident scores to signals that lack historical backing.
screener.a_proofGateReq1: Minimum 15 resolved historical trades for that pattern + instrument combination
screener.a_proofGateReq2: Win rate of 45% or higher across those trades
screener.a_proofGateProven: PROVEN — receives full 4-agent composite scoring (Analyst, Risk, Timing, Portfolio) with TAKE/WATCH/SKIP verdict
screener.a_proofGateUnproven: UNPROVEN — displayed in the Emerging Signals section. Analyst and Composite columns show '—'. Can be sent to Pattern Lab for deeper investigation
screener.a_proofGateEstimated: ESTIMATED — when per-symbol data is insufficient but pattern-level aggregates exist, the Analyst agent uses pooled statistics. Scores are tagged 'Est.' to indicate reduced confidence
screener.a_proofGateNote: Dynamic weight redistribution: When specific scoring factors lack data (e.g., no volume data for an instrument), the engine redistributes those weights proportionally to data-present factors rather than penalizing the score. This prevents artificial score depression for instruments with partial data coverage.
screener.q_gradingVsEdge: What is the difference between quality grades and edge quality?
screener.a_gradingVsEdgeIntro: ChartingPath separates two independent evaluation dimensions that are often conflated on other platforms:
screener.a_gradingDimension1Desc: Measures how well-formed the pattern is based on 9 structural, volume, and context factors. A well-formed pattern has clean geometry, volume confirmation, and trend alignment. Grades answer: 'Is this a textbook pattern?'
screener.a_gradingDimension2Desc: Measures whether the pattern × instrument × timeframe combination actually makes money historically. Based on resolved outcome data with sample sizes. Edge quality answers: 'Does this setup have positive expectancy?'
screener.a_gradingVsEdgeNote: Critical insight: A perfectly formed A-grade pattern on an instrument with no historical edge is still a poor trade. Conversely, a B-grade pattern on a high-edge combination may be excellent. Always cross-reference grades with Edge Atlas or Agent Scoring data before acting on any signal.
screener.q_knownLimitations: What are the known limitations of ChartingPath's evaluation?
screener.a_limitationsIntro: We believe in full transparency. Here are current limitations we're actively working to address:
screener.a_limit1: Survivorship Bias: Historical backtests use currently-listed tickers. Delisted stocks are not included, which may slightly inflate win rates for stock patterns.
screener.a_limit2: Benchmark Alpha: Edge Atlas rankings show absolute returns, not risk-adjusted alpha relative to a benchmark (e.g., S&P 500). A pattern with 15% annualized return is less impressive if SPY returned 12%.
screener.a_limit3: Regime Transitions: While we tag each trade's regime at entry (trend × volatility), we don't yet predict regime transitions — the model is reactive, not predictive.
screener.a_limit4: Intraday Microstructure: Execution assumes bar-close signal, next-bar-open fill. Real-world intraday slippage on illiquid instruments may exceed our estimates.

automation.a_whatIsAutoPaperIntro: Auto Paper Trading automatically opens simulated (paper) trades when one of your alerts detects a matching live pattern. It lets you track how your alert setups would perform in real-time — without risking any real money.
automation.a_howItWorksTitle: How It Works — Step by Step
automation.a_step1: You create an alert for a specific symbol + pattern + timeframe (e.g., AAPL Double Bottom 1D)
automation.a_step2: The platform scans markets every 15 minutes for live pattern detections
automation.a_step3: When a pattern matches your alert, check-alert-matches triggers automatically
automation.a_step4: If Auto Paper Trade is enabled, a paper trade is opened with calculated position sizing
automation.a_step5: The trade appears in your Paper Trading panel on the dashboard with an [Auto] badge
automation.a_noRealMoneyDesc: No real money is involved. This is purely a simulation to validate your alert setups before committing real capital.
automation.q_howToEnable: How do I enable Auto Paper Trading?
automation.a_howToEnableIntro: Enabling auto paper trading is simple:
automation.a_enable1: Go to Members → Alerts and create or edit an alert
automation.a_enable2: Toggle the 'Auto Paper Trade' switch to ON
automation.a_enable3: Set your risk percentage (default: 1% of paper portfolio balance)
automation.a_enable4: Save the alert — trades will be opened automatically when patterns match
automation.a_enableNote: Your paper portfolio starts with a $100,000 simulated balance. You can track all trades in the Paper Trading tab on your dashboard's right sidebar.
automation.q_positionSizing: How is the position size calculated?
automation.a_positionSizingIntro: Position sizing follows professional risk management principles, based on the distance between the entry price and the stop-loss level.
automation.a_formula: Quantity = (Portfolio Balance × Risk %) / |Entry Price − Stop Loss Price|
automation.a_sizing1: Default risk is 1% of your paper portfolio balance (configurable up to 5%)
automation.a_sizing2: Stop loss and take profit are set automatically based on the detected pattern's structure
automation.a_sizing3: A single position cannot exceed 50% of your portfolio to prevent over-concentration
automation.q_whereToTrack: Where can I see my paper trades?
automation.a_whereToTrackIntro: Paper trades are displayed in the Paper Trading tab on your dashboard's right sidebar, alongside Watchlist and Alerts.
automation.a_track1: Portfolio balance, total P&L, and win rate at a glance
automation.a_track2: Open positions with real-time P&L and an [Auto] badge for automated trades
automation.a_track3: Closed trade history with outcome details in an expandable accordion
automation.a_track4: Click any position to navigate the main chart to that symbol
automation.q_duplicateTrades: Will it open duplicate trades on the same symbol?
automation.a_duplicateTradesDesc: No. The system enforces a 'one open trade per symbol' rule. If you already have an open paper trade on AAPL, a new AAPL detection will be skipped. This prevents overexposure and keeps your paper portfolio realistic.
automation.a_webhookIntro: Signal Webhooks allow you to relay ChartingPath's pattern detections to external trading platforms like TradingView, 3Commas, or your own custom bot via HTTPS POST requests.

importGuide.title: Platform Import Guides
importGuide.subtitle: Step-by-step instructions for importing your exported scripts

tv.step1Desc: In TradingView, click the 'Pine Editor' tab at the bottom of the chart. This opens the script editor where you can paste your code.
tv.step2Desc: Click 'Open' → 'New blank indicator' or 'New blank strategy' depending on your export type. Clear any default code.
tv.step3Desc: Copy the entire exported script and paste it into the editor. Make sure to replace all default content.
tv.step3Tip: Use Ctrl+A to select all existing code before pasting to ensure clean replacement.
tv.step4Desc: Click the green 'Add to Chart' button. For strategies, this will also add it to the Strategy Tester.
tv.step4Warning: Check the chart timeframe matches your pattern's timeframe before adding.
tv.step5Desc: Confirm the dynamic SL/TP lines appear on the chart. Check if the SL breach warning is displayed (orange background).
tv.step5Tip: The script enters at current market price and recalculates levels to maintain your R:R ratio.

mt4.step1Desc: In MT4, press F4 or go to Tools → MetaQuotes Language Editor. This opens the IDE for editing EA files.
mt4.step2Desc: Click File → New → Expert Advisor (template). Name it to match your pattern. Click Next through the wizard.
mt4.step3Desc: Select all default code (Ctrl+A) and paste your exported MQL4 script. Save the file (Ctrl+S).
mt4.step3Warning: Ensure the file is saved in the 'MQL4/Experts' folder.
mt4.step4Desc: Press F7 to compile. Check the 'Errors' tab at the bottom - it should show '0 errors'. Warnings are usually okay.
mt4.step5Desc: In MT4, refresh the Navigator (Ctrl+N), find your EA under 'Expert Advisors', and drag it onto the correct chart.
mt4.step5Tip: Enable 'Allow live trading' in the EA settings dialog and ensure AutoTrading is on (button in toolbar).
mt4.step6Desc: Check the 'Experts' tab for entry confirmation logs. The EA will show SL breach warnings if applicable.
mt4.step6Warning: Test on a demo account first. Verify the instrument and timeframe match your pattern.

mt5.step1Desc: In MT5, press F4 or go to Tools → MetaQuotes Language Editor. MT5's editor has enhanced debugging features.
mt5.step2Desc: Click File → New → Expert Advisor (template). The MQL5 wizard offers more options - defaults are fine for our scripts.
mt5.step3Desc: Select all and paste your exported MQL5 script. The CTrade include is handled automatically.
mt5.step3Tip: MQL5 scripts use the CTrade class for cleaner order handling.
mt5.step4Desc: Press F7 to compile. MQL5 has stricter type checking - ensure 0 errors before proceeding.
mt5.step4Warning: If you see 'Trade.mqh not found', ensure your MT5 installation has the standard library.
mt5.step5Desc: In MT5 Navigator, find your EA and drag to chart. MT5 supports both netting and hedging modes - the script handles both.
mt5.step6Desc: Click the 'Algo Trading' button in the toolbar (must be green). Also enable in EA settings: 'Allow Algo Trading'.
mt5.step6Tip: MT5 requires explicit algo trading permission at both platform and EA level.
mt5.step7Desc: Check the 'Experts' and 'Journal' tabs for execution logs. Positions will appear in the 'Trade' tab.
mt5.step7Warning: Always verify on demo first. Symbol naming may differ between brokers (e.g., EURUSD vs EURUSD.pro).

glossary.winRate: Der Prozentsatz der Trades, die im Gewinn enden. Eine 60%-Gewinnrate bedeutet, dass 6 von 10 Trades Gewinner sind.
glossary.expectancy: Durchschnittliche Rendite pro Trade in R-Vielfachen. Positive Erwartung bedeutet, dass die Strategie langfristig profitabel ist.
glossary.sharpe: Risikoadjustierte Renditekennzahl. Höher ist besser. Über 1,0 ist gut, über 2,0 ist ausgezeichnet.
glossary.profitFactor: Bruttogewinne geteilt durch Bruttoverluste. Über 1,0 ist profitabel. Über 2,0 ist stark.
glossary.maxDrawdown: Der größte Rückgang vom Hoch zum Tief im Portfoliowert. Misst das Worst-Case-Verlustszenario.
glossary.riskReward: Verhältnis zwischen potenziellem Verlust und potenziellem Gewinn. Ein R:R von 1:2 bedeutet, 1€ zu riskieren, um 2€ zu gewinnen.
glossary.sampleSize: Anzahl historischer Trades zur Berechnung von Statistiken. Größere Stichproben (50+) liefern zuverlässigere Ergebnisse.
glossary.grade: Gesamtqualitätsbewertung des Signals, die Mustererkennung, Volumenbestätigung und Trendausrichtung kombiniert.
glossary.timeframe: Der Zeitraum, den jede Kerze darstellt (z.B. 1H = 1 Stunde, 1D = 1 Tag).
glossary.takeProfit: Der Zielpreis, bei dem ein gewinnender Trade geschlossen wird.
glossary.stopLoss: Das Preisniveau, bei dem ein verlierender Trade automatisch geschlossen wird.
glossary.paperTrading: Simulierter Handel mit virtuellem Geld zum Üben von Strategien ohne finanzielles Risiko.
glossary.signalAge: Wie lange das Muster zuerst erkannt wurde. Frischere Signale sind tendenziell handelbarer.
glossary.barsToOutcome: Durchschnittliche Anzahl von Kerzen vom Einstieg bis zum Erreichen des Ziels oder Stop-Loss.
glossary.cagr: Jährliche Wachstumsrate — die geglättete jährliche Rendite über einen bestimmten Zeitraum.
glossary.sortino: Wie Sharpe, bestraft aber nur die Abwärtsvolatilität.
glossary.annualizedReturn: Geschätzte Jahresrendite bei kontinuierlichem Handel der Strategie.
glossary.tradesPerYear: Geschätzte Anzahl von Handelsmöglichkeiten pro Jahr für dieses Muster/Zeitrahmen.
glossary.balance: Aktueller Paper-Trading-Portfoliowert einschließlich Anfangskapital plus realisierte Gewinne und Verluste.
glossary.totalPnl: Gesamtgewinn und -verlust — die Summe aller geschlossenen Trade-Ergebnisse.
glossary.rMultiple: Rendite als Vielfaches des anfänglichen Risikos. Ein +2R-Trade bedeutet, dass Sie das 2-fache Ihres Risikos verdient haben.

aboutPage.heroSubtitle: ChartingPath was built on one uncomfortable truth: most traders lose not because they lack discipline — but because nobody ever showed them which patterns actually work.
aboutPage.problemP1Before: Every trading platform shows you chart patterns. TradingView shows you a Bull Flag. Your broker shows you a Head and Shoulders. But none of them answer the only question that matters:
aboutPage.problemP2: We searched for a tool that could tell us — with real data — whether a Descending Triangle on EURUSD at the 4H timeframe had a positive expectancy over the last 5 years. It didn't exist. So we built it.
aboutPage.beliefDataDesc: Every pattern claim on ChartingPath is backed by real historical outcomes — not textbook theory, not YouTube heuristics. 320,000+ trades don't lie.
aboutPage.beliefEdgeDesc: We don't sell signals. We don't promise returns. We give you the statistical foundation to develop your own edge — and the tools to validate it before risking a dollar.
aboutPage.beliefTransparencyDesc: Our Edge Atlas is public. Anyone can see which patterns work and which don't. We have nothing to hide because the data speaks for itself.
aboutPage.whatItIsTagline: A pattern intelligence platform — not a signal service.
aboutPage.whatItIsP1: There's an important difference. A signal service tells you what to trade. A pattern intelligence platform shows you what works, why it works, and how often — then lets you decide.
aboutPage.whatItIsP2Before: ChartingPath scans 800+ instruments across forex, crypto, stocks, and commodities every hour. It detects 17 chart patterns. It backtests each one against years of real price data. And it gives every signal a statistical verdict:
aboutPage.howScoringDesc: Every live pattern detection passes through four AI agents simultaneously. The Analyst agent evaluates historical win rate and expectancy. The Risk agent calculates reward-to-risk and Kelly criterion. The Timing agent checks trend alignment and economic event proximity. The Portfolio agent scores basket fit. The result is a composite score from 0–100 and a TAKE / WATCH / SKIP verdict — automatically, every hour.
aboutPage.whyMattersDesc: Pattern trading is fundamentally about supply and demand dynamics forming in recognisable geometric shapes. When a Bull Flag forms, it means demand is consolidating after absorbing supply — and historically, that consolidation resolves upward X% of the time. ChartingPath quantifies that X. For every pattern. On every instrument. Across every timeframe.

aboutPage.moatP1: Every time a trader paper trades a signal on ChartingPath, the outcome feeds back into the scoring model. Not theoretical outcomes — real ones, validated against actual price movement. This creates a proprietary dataset that no competitor can replicate without starting over.
aboutPage.moatP2: TradingView can copy our UI. Bloomberg can copy our patterns. Nobody can copy five years of user-validated pattern outcomes tied to real market conditions.
aboutPage.profileSystematicDesc: You already have a process. You want data to validate it — not replace it. ChartingPath gives you the statistical foundation to know which patterns have genuine edge in your markets before you risk capital.
aboutPage.profilePatternDesc: You've been trading Bull Flags and Double Bottoms for years — mostly on gut feel. ChartingPath tells you which ones actually worked, on which instruments, at which timeframes. Finally: proof.
aboutPage.profilePropDesc: You're paying for challenge accounts hoping your strategy survives the drawdown rules. ChartingPath lets you validate your pattern strategy against 320,000+ historical outcomes before you spend another dollar on a challenge fee.
aboutPage.visionDesc: The future we're building is one where any trader — regardless of experience — can answer the question "does my strategy actually work?" in under 60 seconds. Where pattern outcomes are transparent, verifiable, and continuously improving. Where the gap between retail traders and institutional desks narrows — not because retail traders got more capital, but because they finally got the same quality of information.
